title: Nonlinear Dynamics, Stability, and Control · Mixed Practice Return
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving controlled nonlinear systems. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a reproducible dynamical systems computational study after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate phase portraits in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive equilibria stability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute linearization systems in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Feedback Control applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Communicate feedback control in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
